content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA, an element AiA_iAi​ (1<i<∣A∣)(1 \lt i \lt |A|)(1<i<∣A∣) is said to be a peak if Ai>Ai−1A_i \gt A_{i-1}Ai​>Ai−1​ and Ai>Ai+1A_i \gt A_{i+1}Ai​>Ai+1​.

Note that the first and last elements are never peaks.

You are given a permutation PPP of length NNN, and an empty set SSS. In one operation, you can do the following:


Remove an element PiP_iPi​ such that PiP_iPi​ is a peak, and insert it into the set SSS. Note that subsequent operations are done on the modified PPP.
Let GGG denote the set of all possible sets SSS that can be obtained by doing any number of finite operations on the initial permutation PPP.

Your task is to find the sum of elements across all sets in GGG.

Formally, if we define F(X)F(X)F(X) to be the sum of all elements in a set XXX, find ∑X∈GF(X)\sum_{X \in G} F(X)∑X∈G​F(X).

Since this number may be large, print the answer modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Note that a permutation of length NNN consists of all elements from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input contains the number of test cases TTT.
The first line of each test case contains NNN, the length of the permutation.
The next line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2,\ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the sum of elements across all sets modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.
**Subtask 1 (20 points):** 1≤N≤10001 \leq N \leq 10001≤N≤1000 (Sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases won't exceed 10710^7107)

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only possible set we can obtain is {3}\{3\}{3}.
Test case 222: There are no possible peaks which can be removed.
Test case 333: We can obtain the sets {4},{5},{4,5},{3,4,5}\{4\}, \{5\}, \{4, 5\}, \{3, 4, 5\}{4},{5},{4,5},{3,4,5}. The sum of values across these sets is 4+5+4+5+3+4+5=304+5+4+5+3+4+5 = 304+5+4+5+3+4+5=30
